title: GitHub suddenly rejected my SSH key (the fix was a .pub file?!)
excerpt: Today, git pull stopped working on my main laptop. Permission denied (publickey), out of nowhere. I had changed nothing, the key was still registered on GitHub, and pulling the same repository from another laptop (with another key) worked fine.
content_html:
1 minute read

Today, git pull stopped working on my main laptop. Permission denied (publickey), out of nowhere. I had changed nothing, the key was still registered on GitHub, and pulling the same repository from another laptop (with another key) worked fine.

The rabbit hole

I’ll spare you the full troubleshooting session1. The key was mathematically sound (openssl rsa -check said RSA key ok), the signature algorithm was the modern rsa-sha2-512, my ~/.ssh/config was clean, and GitHub’s status page was all green. Still: Permission denied.

The fix turned out to be embarrassingly small. My ~/.ssh/github_rsa has lived without a companion .pub file since I re-installed my laptop. Generating one made authentication work again:

$> ssh-keygen -y -f ~/.ssh/github_rsa > ~/.ssh/github_rsa.pub

I did not believe it either, so I tested it six times with the .pub file and six times without. Twelve out of twelve: no .pub – rejected, .pub – accepted.

Why on earth would that matter?

Because the .pub file changes which authentication flow OpenSSH uses. With a .pub file present, the client first probes (offers the public key, waits for the server’s OK) and only then signs. With only a private key, OpenSSH skips the probe and sends a fully signed authentication request directly. Both flows are perfectly legal according to RFC 4252, and a stock sshd accepts both.

GitHub, as of today, apparently does not.

Did something change on GitHub’s side?

My money says yes. The server banner in my debug logs reads 6a2c000, where GitHub’s SSH frontend has historically identified itself as babeld-<hash>. That smells like new server software – one that rejects direct-signed publickey requests. It would explain perfectly why everything worked in the morning and nothing worked an hour later, on a machine where nothing changed.

Has anyone else hit this? Do you know what 6a2c000 is? Let me know – and in the meantime: make sure your private keys have their .pub siblings.

  1. Everything from ssh -vvv archaeology, via openssl key validation, to a controlled 12-trial experiment. ↩

content_html_zh:
1分钟读完

今天，git pull 停止在我的主笔记本电脑上工作。 权限被突然拒绝（公钥）。 我没有做任何更改，密钥仍然在 GitHub 上注册，并且从另一台笔记本电脑（使用另一个密钥）提取相同的存储库工作正常。

兔子洞

我将省去您完整的故障排除会话1。 密钥在数学上是合理的（openssl rsa -check 说 RSA 密钥没问题），签名算法是现代的 rsa-sha2-512，我的 ~/.ssh/config 是干净的，GitHub 的状态页面是全绿色的。 仍然：许可被拒绝。

事实证明，修复的效果小得令人尴尬。 自从我重新安装笔记本电脑以来，我的 ~/.ssh/github_rsa 一直没有配套的 .pub 文件。 生成一个使身份验证再次工作：

$> ssh-keygen -y -f ~/.ssh/github_rsa > ~/.ssh/github_rsa.pub

我也不相信，所以我使用 .pub 文件测试了六次，不使用 .pub 文件测试了六次。 十二个中的十二个：没有 .pub – 被拒绝，.pub – 被接受。

这到底为什么重要？

因为 .pub 文件更改了 OpenSSH 使用的身份验证流程。 有了 .pub 文件，客户端首先进行探测（提供公钥，等待服务器的 OK），然后才进行签名。 仅使用私钥，OpenSSH 就会跳过探测并直接发送完全签名的身份验证请求。 根据 RFC 4252，这两种流量都是完全合法的，并且普通 sshd 接受这两种流量。

截至今天，GitHub 显然还没有。

GitHub 方面有什么变化吗？

我的钱说是的。 我的调试日志中的服务器横幅显示为 6a2c000，其中 GitHub 的 SSH 前端历史上将自己标识为 babeld-<hash>。 这听起来像是新的服务器软件——一种拒绝直接签名的公钥请求的软件。 这可以完美地解释为什么在一台没有任何变化的机器上，早上一切正常，一小时后什么也不起作用。

还有其他人打过这个吗？ 你知道6a2c000是什么吗？ 让我知道 - 同时：确保您的私钥有它们的 .pub 同级密钥。

  1. 从 ssh -vvv 考古、通过 openssl 密钥验证到受控 12 次试验的一切。 ↩
